sentence: Production decode teams rarely fail for lack of framework features---they fail because compiler and kernel schedules violate residency, launch, and bandwidth contracts on the SKU they actually ship .
title: PyTorch 2.0: The Journey to Bringing Compiler Technologies to the Core of PyTorch (Keynote)

sentence: This chapter continues the book's problem-first thread: name the contradiction, trace it to hardware limits, then show how passes and megakernels recover SLO on GPU, CPU, and NPU fleets .

title: PyTorch 2.0: The Journey to Bringing Compiler Technologies to the Core of PyTorch (Keynote)

sentence: Teams that treat parallel as independent knobs routinely ship kernels that win microbenchmarks yet miss decode SLOs on real fleets .
title: Flash-Decoding for Long-Context Inference

sentence: The hardware--software contract here binds parallel to measurable bytes/token and launches/token outcomes across GPU, CPU, and NPU targets .
title: CPU-GPU Workload Distribution during Throughput-Oriented LLM Inference on Single-GPU Systems

sentence: YiRage lowers the same constraints as explicit legality checks before codegen, so illegal fusion fails in CI rather than in customer dashboards .
title: YiRage: Multi-Backend LLM Inference Optimization

sentence: Industrial readers should map each paragraph to the Chapter~3 constraint matrix and record fleet measurements alongside compiler flags .
